Significance Testing

Each wave is compared against the baseline wave using a z-test (for proportions) or t-test (for means) at the 95% confidence level (p<0.05).

▲ Significantly higher than baseline
▼ Significantly lower than baseline
— No significant difference

Minimum base size: n=30. Results with low base sizes are flagged.

Prepared by The Research LampPost | Powered by Turas Analytics

Tracking Report Help

Report Tabs

  • Summary — Key findings and methodology notes
  • Metrics by Segment — Explore one metric at a time with segment chips and chart toggle
  • Segment Overview — Full crosstab table with all metrics
  • Added Slides — Insert custom slides with images and commentary
  • Pinned Views — Save and compare specific metric views

Significance Indicators

  • ↑ Significant increase
  • ↓ Significant decrease
  • → No significant change

Segment Chips

Click segment chips to show or hide individual segments. Total is shown by default.

Trend Annotations

Click a data point on any chart to add a contextual annotation (e.g., 'Campaign launched'). Annotations appear as labelled markers on the chart.

Metric Comparison

Enable 'Compare' mode on the Metrics by Segment tab to overlay up to 3 metrics on a single chart for correlation analysis.

Export

Export tables as CSV or Excel. Export charts and metrics as high-resolution PNG slides. Copy charts to clipboard for pasting into presentations.
